components.name: amorphous chitin (poly-N-acetylglucosamine; hydrolyzed to GlcNAc)
components.evidence: mineral medium Laguna Chitin (LC), pH 7.0 ... supplemented with amorphous chitin (2 g L-1, final concentration) ... Chitin from shrimp shells was added at the final concentration 5 g l-1 to serve as growth substrate ... N-acetylglucosamine (GlcNAc) was found in supernatants

media.base_medium: ONR7a artificial seawater medium (Dyksterhouse et al. 1995; DSMZ Medium 950), made hypersaline (240 g/L total salinity) by raising NaCl to 200 g/L and MgCl2 to 30 g/L, buffered with 5 g/L HEPES at pH 7.0, plus Pfennig & Lippert (1966) acidic trace-metal solution and vitamin mix, 50 mg/L yeast extract, and amorphous chitin as carbon source.

sources_chased.what: paper main text (PMC7443923) - LC salinity, Na+/Mg2+, chitin
SI Appendix (pnas.2007232117.sapp.pdf) - full LC recipe & base-medium citation
added: NaCl 200 / MgCl2 30 / NH4Cl 0.5 / HEPES 5 g/L modifications

sources_chased.what: base recipe cited as ref 45 (Dyksterhouse 1995, ONR7a medium)
trace-metal + vitamin stocks cited as ref 46 (Pfennig & Lippert 1966)
sources_chased.how: DSMZ MediaDive solutions S3087 (trace) and S3031 (vitamins)

missing_before: sulfate/phosphate/K+/Ca2+ were present but underspecified; NaBr, NaF, SrCl2, EDTA, buffers (HEPES/TAPSO) newly enumerated
media.notes: LC medium is a hypersaline (240 g/L) derivative of ONR7a: base ONR7a salts + NaCl to 200 g/L, MgCl2 to 30 g/L, NH4Cl 0.5 g/L, HEPES buffer to pH 7.0, plus 1 mL/L each of the Pfennig & Lippert (1966) acidic trace-metal solution and vitamin mix, 50 mg/L yeast extract, and amorphous chitin (hydrolyzed extracellularly to GlcNAc) as sole carbon source. No chemical reductant is added; the host is aerobic/facultative and the coculture self-generates microoxic conditions in sealed serum bottles (hence oxygen=facultative, microoxic optimum). Trace/vitamin per-L amounts are the DSMZ rendering of the cited Pfennig & Lippert 1966 stocks; minor amount discrepancies exist between DSMZ renderings but the element/vitamin set is consistent.
